data.title: Wheat Futures Rebound from Five-Year Low
data.description:
Wheat futures rose toward $5.20 per bushel, rebounding from a five-year low, supported by technical buying and signs of stronger export demand.

Traders noted that the rebound came despite abundant global supply, particularly from Northern Hemisphere harvests.

The price bounce was attributed in part to strong US export sales, which exceeded market expectations, and were seen as driven by the attractiveness of cheaper prices.

Weakness in the US dollar also contributed to making US wheat more competitive in international markets.

However, the market remains pressured by ample supply, which had triggered a steep sell-off earlier in the week.

data.title: The 30-Year Bond Auction Went Poorly. Yields Are Popping Higher. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Karishma Vanjani

An auction of government bonds that expires in three decades saw weak demand on Thursday, in an ominous sign for the Treasury market.

The Treasury Department sold $25 billion worth of 30-year debt at a yield of 4.813%, which was more than two basis points above the yield's level at the pre-bidding deadline. Higher rates at auctions signal that demand is weak, as the Treasury has to entice investors to buy U.S. debt. One basis point is equal to 0.01%.

Yields leapt higher in response, with the yield on the 30-year Treasury note rising to 4.829%. Earlier Thursday, the yield had been below the level it settled at on Wednesday. Higher yields means prices of Treasuries have started falling as investors feel disappointed by the results.

The Treasury's auction of debt expiring in 10-years also saw poor demand on Wednesday. Both auctions are widely watched; 10-year debt forms the basis of rates around the world, including for mortgages and corporate debt while 30-year debt is routinely bought by pension fund and insurers.

Thursday marked the third consecutive weak auction this week: Tuesday's auction of 3-year debt also went poorly.

This is breaking news. Please check back for more analysis soon.

Write to Karishma Vanjani at karishma.vanjani@dowjones.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Canadian dollar pulls back from 10-day high ahead of key jobs report
data.description:
Key points:
  • Canadian dollar dips 0.1% against the greenback
  • Touches an earlier 10-day high at 1.3719
  • Price of U.S. oil decreases 0.5%
  • Bond yields edge lower across the curve

The Canadian dollar edged lower against its U.S. counterpart on Thursday, pulling back from an earlier 10-day high, as oil prices dipped and ahead of domestic employment data that could guide expectations for Bank of Canada interest rate cuts.

The loonie was trading 0.1% lower at 1.3753 per U.S. dollar, or 72.71 U.S. cents, after touching its strongest intraday level since July 28 at 1.3719.

"Tomorrow's employment release is the main event: we expect a sharp slowdown in job growth and a tick higher in the unemployment rate, which should keep the Bank of Canada cautious and cap further gains for the loonie," strategists at Monex Europe said in a note.

Canada's employment report for July, due on Friday, is expected to show a more moderate jobs gain of 13,500 after the economy added 83,100 jobs in June.

Last week, the BoC opened the door to resuming interest rate cuts if the upward price pressures from trade disruptions are contained. Investors see a near 30% chance the central bank eases next month. (0#CADIRPR)

"Everyone is trying to figure out the impact of tariffs, the outlook on the Canadian economy," said Rahim Madhavji, president at KnightsbridgeFX.com. "Oil prices are lower which is also a headwind for CAD."

The price of oil , one of Canada's major exports, fell 0.5% to $64.03 a barrel as expectations rose for a diplomatic end to the war in Ukraine.

Domestic data was upbeat. The seasonally adjusted Ivey Purchasing Managers Index (PMI) rose to 55.8 last month from 53.3 in June, posting its highest level since July 2024.

Still, Canadian bond yields edged lower across the curve. The 10-year (CA10YT=RR) was down nearly one basis point at 3.400%.

data.title: Wix com Is Maintained at Sector Outperform by Scotiabank
Target Hospitality Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Target Hospitality Corp., a leading provider of specialty rental and hospitality services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational metrics, reflecting the company's performance amidst changes in government contracts and market conditions.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $131.5 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous period due to changes in government contracts and reduced specialty rental income.

Gross Profit: $17.6 million, indicating a significant decline compared to the prior period, primarily due to increased costs in services and specialty rental.

Operating Income (Loss): $(18.0) million, a substantial decrease from the previous period, driven by higher service costs and depreciation expenses.

Net Income (Loss): $(21.4) million, reflecting a downturn from the previous period, impacted by the termination of key government contracts and increased interest expenses.

Net Income (Loss) Per Share - Basic: $(0.22), highlighting the impact of reduced revenue and increased costs on shareholder returns.

Net Income (Loss) Per Share - Diluted: $(0.22), consistent with the basic EPS, indicating no dilution effect due to the net loss.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates in three main segments: HFS – South, Government, and WHS. HFS – South focuses on specialty rental and hospitality services in Texas and New Mexico, Government segment deals with contracts in Texas, and WHS is involved in construction phase contracts with Lithium Nevada.

Geographical Performance: The HFS – South segment, primarily located in Texas and New Mexico, generated $72.2 million in revenue for the six months ended June 30, 2025, showing a slight decrease compared to the previous year. The Government segment, also in Texas, saw a significant drop in revenue to $33.2 million due to the termination of the Pecos Children’s Center contract.

New Contract Developments: The Dilley Immigration Processing Center (DIPC) contract was activated in March 2025, replacing the previous STFRC contract. This new contract is expected to generate over $246 million in revenue over its five-year term, with a ramp-up period based on utilization.

Operational Adjustments: The company is actively re-marketing assets from the terminated Pecos Children’s Center contract, which previously supported up to 6,000 individuals, to meet customer demand across its segments.

Future Outlook: The DIPC contract is anticipated to provide stable revenue with a fixed minimum amount over its term, and the company is focusing on optimizing asset utilization and exploring growth opportunities in its operating segments.

SEC Filing: Target Hospitality Corp. [ TH ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: KELLY SERVICES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Kelly Services Inc., a global leader in workforce solutions, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing significant financial and operational achievements. The report highlights the company's robust performance driven by strategic acquisitions and improved operational efficiency.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue from services: $1,101.8 million, increased 4.2% from the prior year primarily driven by the acquisition of MRP.
  • Gross profit: $225.5 million, increased 5.5% largely driven by the acquisition of MRP, with a gross profit rate of 20.5%, up 30 basis points.
  • Earnings from operations: $22.2 million, increased 81.0% from the prior year, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Net earnings: $19.0 million, increased 314.7% from the prior year, driven by higher gross profit and lower other expenses.
  • Basic earnings per share: $0.52, reflecting significant growth compared to the prior year.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $0.52, showing substantial improvement from the previous year's $0.12.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The Enterprise Talent Management (ETM) segment, which includes the Sevenstep business acquired as part of the MRP acquisition, saw a decrease in revenue excluding the acquisition by 5.1% year-over-year.
    • The Science, Engineering & Technology (SET) segment experienced a 19.4% increase in revenue, primarily driven by the acquisition of MRP staffing and outcome-based solutions businesses.
    • The Education segment reported a 5.6% increase in revenue, attributed to improved fill rates and stable demand.
  • Geographical Performance:
    • The Americas region, including the United States, showed strong performance with revenue increasing to $1,073.1 million in the second quarter of 2025, up from $1,034.2 million in the same period of 2024.
    • The Europe and Asia-Pacific regions also contributed to the overall revenue growth, with the Asia-Pacific region showing a notable increase in revenue.
  • Sales Units:
    • The ETM segment reported a decrease in staffing services revenue by 7.7% due to lower hours volume, particularly with large customers.
    • The SET segment, excluding the acquisition, saw an 8.5% decrease in revenue from services, driven by declines in hours volume in staffing specialties.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on integrating MRP’s portfolio with existing SET businesses and aligning sales, recruiting, and functional teams as part of a redefined go-to-market strategy. The integration of the Sevenstep talent solutions business with ETM’s talent solutions business is progressing, with a focus on leveraging MRP’s technology stack to drive efficiency.

SEC Filing: KELLY SERVICES INC [ KELYA ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Gold Rises on Rate Cut Expectations — Market Talk
data.description: Gold futures gain ground, with jobless claims rising more than expected this week and the Bank of England cutting rates for a fifth time this year. The developments have investors thinking a rate cut in the U.S. will come soon. "The weak data spurred markets to increase bets that the Fed will cut rates at its September meeting and beyond," says ADM Investor Services in a note. Front-month gold futures rise 0.6% to $3,400.30 a troy ounce. That makes it four out of the past five sessions that gold finished higher.(kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: ASTRONICS CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Astronics Corp, a leading provider of advanced technologies for the global aerospace and defense industries, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial improvements and robust operational performance, driven by strong demand in the Aerospace segment and strategic initiatives to enhance efficiency.

Financial Highlights

Sales: Astronics Corp reported sales of $410.6 million, primarily driven by the continued strength in demand from the Aerospace segment, especially within the Commercial Transport market.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $113.7 million, reflecting a gross margin of 27.7%, an improvement from 26.9% in the prior year. This increase is attributed to higher sales volume.

Income from Operations: Income from operations rose to $17.9 million, up from $9.2 million in the previous year, indicating enhanced operational efficiency.

Net Income: Astronics Corp reported a net income of $10.8 million, a significant turnaround from a net loss of $1.6 million in the prior year. This improvement is due to stronger profitability and lower interest expenses.

Diluted EPS: The diluted earnings per share (EPS) increased to $0.30, compared to a loss of $0.05 per share in the prior year, reflecting the company's improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

Aerospace Segment Performance: The Aerospace segment experienced a 13.0% increase in sales, driven by strong demand in the Commercial Transport and Military Aircraft markets. This growth was primarily attributed to increased demand for cabin power and inflight entertainment & connectivity products.

Test Systems Segment Performance: The Test Systems segment saw a decline in sales due to revisions of estimated costs to complete certain long-term mass transit Test contracts, resulting in reduced revenue recognized during the period.

Product Line Revenue Breakdown: Within the Aerospace segment, Electrical Power & Motion products generated $195.2 million, Lighting & Safety products $102.7 million, and Avionics $65.3 million in sales, reflecting strong demand across these product lines.

Geographical Performance: Astronics Corp has principal operations in the United States, Canada, and France, with engineering offices in Ukraine and India, indicating a broad geographical footprint supporting its Aerospace and Test Systems segments.

Operational Challenges: The company faced challenges such as supply chain pressures, material availability, and cost increases, which impacted operational efficiency. These challenges were addressed through simplification initiatives and restructuring activities.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in the Aerospace segment driven by new aircraft production and upgrades. The Test Systems segment is expected to benefit from new and follow-on business opportunities, despite current challenges.

Simplification Initiatives: The Aerospace segment undertook simplification activities, including footprint rationalization and portfolio shaping, resulting in charges within the cost of products sold.

Legal Proceedings Impact: The company incurred significant legal expenses related to a patent infringement dispute in the UK, impacting the Aerospace segment's operating profit.

Backlog and Bookings: The company's backlog increased to $645.4 million, with a book-to-bill ratio of 1.11:1 for the first half of 2025, indicating strong future demand.

New Production Launches: The company is expanding its facility, which contributed to higher capital expenditures in the first half of 2025, supporting future growth in production capacity.

SEC Filing: ASTRONICS CORP [ ATRO ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: US Education Department investigating Baltimore schools on anti-Semitism allegations
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Education said on Thursday that it is investigating Baltimore City Public Schools over allegations of tolerating anti-Semitic incidents.

The investigation was ignited by a complaint from the Anti-Defamation League, a Jewish advocacy group, against school leaders. The Department of Education said in a written statement that its investigation will look at numerous alleged incidents of anti-Semitic discrimination and harassment by teachers and non-Jewish students against Jewish students.

It's the latest investigation that the Trump administration has launched into schools and universities across the country in response to what the administration calls a wave of "radical left" policies that have led learning institutions astray. The investigations have mainly focused on issues of anti-Semitism, race and gender, and have targeted a wide array of institutions across the country, from elite Ivy League universities down to elementary schools.

Among the allegations in the Baltimore investigation are that school leaders didn't fire a teacher who allegedly gave Nazi salutes to Jewish students, nor did they reprimand non-Jewish students who allegedly performed Nazi salutes on the playground and drew Nazi graffiti on desks and textbooks.

The department statement says that "Jewish students have reported feeling afraid to walk down the hallways alone and are unable to focus on schoolwork."

Sherry Christian, a spokesperson for Baltimore City Public Schools, said in a written statement that she could not comment on the Department of Education's investigation, citing pending litigation. But she wrote that school leaders "unequivocally reject antisemitism and hate in any form."

Baltimore City Public Schools came under scrutiny when anti-Semitic messages and images were shown during a July 22 Board of School Commissioners hybrid online-in person meeting, which school officials denounced and blamed on an "unauthorized individual" who accessed the online portion of the meeting and displayed the offensive images.

"City Schools stands in solidarity with our Jewish community and with all who seek to create safe, inclusive, and respectful learning environments," Christian said. "We have condemned hateful behavior and taken action to hold students and staff accountable when our values and policies are violated."

data.title: Nvidia Stock Climbs as Wall Street Hikes Targets Ahead of Earnings Blowout
data.description:
Aug 7 - Nvidia shares rose about 2% Thursday after several major Wall Street firms raised their price targets ahead of the chipmaker's second-quarter earnings, set for Aug. 27.

Analysts from Morgan Stanley , Bank of America and Goldman Sachs pointed to strong demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure and Nvidia's Blackwell platform as key drivers of potential growth. Morgan Stanley now sees shares reaching $200, up from $170.

Bank of America reaffirmed its Buy rating and maintained a $220 target, while Goldman Sachs lifted its target to $200, citing expectations for a beat-and-raise quarter. Analysts flagged investor focus on Blackwell's chip ramp, margin expansion potential, and China's H20 chip rollout.

Estimates compiled by FactSet suggest Nvidia will post earnings of $1 per share on $45.7 billion in revenue. The stock is up nearly 34% year to date.

Nvidia also inched up 1% in premarket trading as optimism builds heading into the earnings call.

Trivago Warns of Softer U.S. Travel Demand Amid Consumer Caution
data.description:
(18:27 GMT) *U.S. Travelers Are Opting for Cheaper Hotels Closer to Home, Trivago CEO Says >TRVG
(18:27 GMT) *Trivago CEO: Too Early to Tell How Recent Price Trends Will Affect Bookings >TRVG
(18:27 GMT) *Travel Demand Remains Solid in Europe, Rest of World, Trivago CEO Says >TRVG
(18:27 GMT) *Trivago to Continue Investing in Advertising, AI to Gain Share, CEO Says >TRVG
(18:27 GMT) Trivago Warns of Softer U.S. Travel Demand Amid Consumer Caution

By Connor Hart

U.S. travelers are hunting for deals and trading down to cheaper hotels closer to home, a trend that could point to economic weakness, Trivago Chief Executive Johannes Thomas said.

While revenue in the travel site's Americas unit rose 10% in the second quarter and search traffic remained strong, the average trip price slipped 1% as consumers traded down to less expensive hotels.

"People are a bit more uncertain and looking at hotels that are a bit more affordable," Thomas said Wednesday. While the trend signals some weakness, it is too early to tell how it will affect future bookings, he added.

The caution among American travellers stands in contrast to elsewhere around the world, where trends generally remain stable.

Revenue in Trivago's European unit climbed 20%, with average trip prices up 10%. Europeans currently feel more confident about spending on travel than Americans, Thomas said, though they remain less likely to book trips to the U.S.

Sales in Trivago's "Rest of World" business jumped 32%, driven by strength in Japan and Turkey. Average booking volumes were largely flat.

Overall, second-quarter revenue rose 17% to EUR139.3 million ($162.4 million), slightly above the EUR138 million analysts expected, according to FactSet.

Thomas said he was encouraged that users with memberships to the site generated about 20% of the top line, calling it a sign of success in Trivago's push to deepen engagement and build loyalty. Logged-in users are 25% more likely to book trips through the company's site, and they more often become repeat customers, he added.

To attract more users, Trivago plans to continue investing in advertising and artificial intelligence. "It's really about building a better product that then allows us to target audiences more effectively and invest more into marketing without losing much profitability," Thomas said.

Trivago's revenue growth continued at a single-digit pace early in the third quarter and is expected to accelerate through the peak summer travel season. The company projects double-digit growth for the period, though at a slower rate than in the second quarter.

It reiterated its full-year outlook for mid-teens revenue growth. Analysts expect EUR533.9 million in sales this year, up 16% from 2024.

The Düsseldorf-based company posted a second-quarter loss of EUR6.5 million, compared with a loss of EUR4.9 million a year earlier. Analysts had expected a EUR6.9 million loss.

Trivago's American depository receipts ticked up 1.3%, to $3.54, in Thursday afternoon trading, extending their 72% gain over the past year.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.description:
• Akebia Therapeutics Inc reported quarterly breakeven results​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -4 cents. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 2 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -4 cents to -1 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 43.1% to $62.47 million from a year ago; analysts expected $47.24 million.

RECOMMENDATIONS

data.title: Consumers Energy, the Principal Subsidiary of CMS Energy, Declares Quarterly Dividend on Preferred Stock
data.description:
JACKSON, Mich., Aug. 7, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — The Board of Directors of Consumers Energy, the principal subsidiary of CMS Energy, has declared a quarterly dividend on the utility's preferred stock.

The following dividend is payable Oct. 1, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on Sept. 2, 2025: $1.125 per share on the $4.50 preferred stock (_pb).

Additional dividend information, including the tax status of Consumers Energy's dividend distributions, can be obtained through the Tax Information section of CMS Energy's website.

CMS Energy is a Michigan-based energy provider featuring Consumers Energy as its primary business. It also owns and operates independent power generation businesses.

For more information on CMS Energy, please visit our website at cmsenergy.com. To sign up for email alert notifications, please visit the Investor Relations section of our website.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/consumers-energy-the-principal-subsidiary-of-cms-energy-declares-quarterly-dividend-on-preferred-stock-302524651.html

SOURCE CMS Energy

data.title: First Majestic Second Quarter 2025 Results Conference Call Details
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - August 7, 2025) - First Majestic Silver Corp. (FSE: FMV) (the "Company" or "First Majestic") will host a conference call and webcast on Thursday, August 14, 2025, at 8:30 a.m. (PT) / 11:30 a.m. (ET) to discuss its second quarter production and earnings results and updated 2025 guidance. Please note this is 30 minutes later than previously announced.

The Company's second quarter results will be announced before markets open on Thursday, August 14, 2025, and will be available at www.firstmajestic.com.

To participate in the conference call, please use the following dial-in numbers:

Canada & USA Toll-Free:+1-833-752-3407
Outside of Canada & USA:+1-647-846-2866
Toll-Free Germany:+49-69-1741-5718
Toll-Free UK:+44-20-3795-9972

Participants should dial-in at least 15 minutes prior to the start of the call to ensure placement in the conference on time.

The live webcast link of the call will be accessible prior to the start of the call directly at this link, Q2 2025 Results Conference Call, as well as on the First Majestic home page at www.firstmajestic.com through the "August 14, 2025 Webcast Link". A webcast archive will be available approximately one hour after the end of the event and will be accessible for three months through the same link as the live event.

A recording of the conference call will be available for telephone replay approximately one hour after the end of the event by calling:

USA & Canada Toll-Free:+1-855-669-9658
Outside of Canada & US:+1-412-317-0088
Access Code:1902689

The telephone replay will be available for seven days following the end of the event.

ABOUT FIRST MAJESTIC

First Majestic is a publicly traded mining company focused on silver and gold production in Mexico and the United States. The Company presently owns and operates four producing underground mines in Mexico: the Los Gatos Silver Mine (the Company holds a 70% interest in the Los Gatos Joint Venture that owns and operates the mine), the Santa Elena Silver/Gold Mine, the San Dimas Silver/Gold Mine, and the La Encantada Silver Mine, as well as a portfolio of development and exploration assets, including the Jerritt Canyon Gold project located in northeastern Nevada, U.S.A.

First Majestic is proud to own and operate its own minting facility, First Mint, LLC, and to offer a portion of its silver production for sale to the public. Bars, ingots, coins and medallions are available for purchase online at http://www.firstmint.com/, at some of the lowest premiums available.

For further information, contact info@firstmajestic.com, visit our website at www.firstmajestic.com or call our toll free number 1.866.529.2807.

FIRST MAJESTIC SILVER CORP.

"signed"

Keith Neumeyer, President & CEO

Forward-looking statements in this news release include, but are not limited to, statements with respect to: the date and time of the Company’s conference call and webcast to discuss its second quarter production and earnings results and updated 2025 guidance; the timing for the Company’s announcement of its second quarter results; the availability of a live webcast of the conference call; the availability of an archived version of the webcast and the duration to access such archived version; and the availability of a recording of the conference call for telephone replay and the duration to access such recording. All statements other than statements of historical fact may be forward‐looking statements. Any forward-looking statements in this news release speak only as of the date hereof.
